reasoning: The underlying divergence is real and large, but the TITLE is false and must not be implemented as written. Decoded the whole window state machine myself. PLNDEL:2743-2754 -> P.WINDW:2755-2801 is a THREE-ZONE machine keyed on |screen pos| vs two level-indexed tables, with FLAG+1 D7 = sign(delta) EOR sign(pos) as the 'reverse?' bit (P.WCHK:2811-2812: D7=0 -> 40$ 'NEED TO REVERSE DIRECTION'). Zone 1, OUTSIDE P.OLIM (2775-2782): default D7 applies -> an outbound plane IS reversed back toward centre. The table's own comment at RBARON.MAC:2939 reads `P.OLIM: .WORD 40 ;RETURN TO CENTER LIMIT`. Zone 2, the MIDDLE band (2789-2792): `LDA I,0FF / STA FLAG+1 ;NO DIRECTION CHANGES` -> free-run, servo speed to P.IDLX. Zone 3, INSIDE P.ILIM (2794-2797): EOR 0FF -> `REVERSE FLAG (HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER)`, table comment at 2945 `;HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER LIMIT`. So the ROM reverses at BOTH windows — they are a matched pair forming the oscillator. The finding's own body concedes the outer inversion exists ('inverted only conditionally'), so the title is self-refuting. VERIFIED CORRECT in the finding: P.INER's verbatim line; the .3WORD macro decode (RBARON.MAC:20-22 = `.WORD 3*.A,...`), giving P.IIDL = [0,30,48,78,C0] hex = [0,48,72,120,192] decimal exactly as claimed, including P.IIDL[0]=0; and that our enemy.ts:152-154 is a bare 2-state wall-bouncer with P_ILIM read exactly once, in spawn() (enemy.ts:124), never in step().
corrections: TITLE IS WRONG — DO NOT 'MOVE THE REVERSAL FROM OUTER TO INNER'. The ROM reverses at BOTH windows: P.OLIM = 'RETURN TO CENTER LIMIT' (RBARON.MAC:2939), P.ILIM = 'HEAD AWAY FROM CENTER LIMIT' (RBARON.MAC:2945). Deleting our outer reversal would be a regression.

The real divergence is the MISSING MIDDLE ZONE and the MISSING SPEED SERVO, not the location of the reversal. We have 2 zones (inside / at-the-wall); the ROM has 3: |pos|>P.OLIM -> reverse toward centre + servo to P.ODLX; P.ILIM<|pos|<P.OLIM -> NO direction change at all, servo to P.IDLX; |pos|<P.ILIM -> reverse away from centre, servo to P.IIDL.

We have no per-zone target-speed table. Decoded (hex region, .2WORD = x2, .3WORD = x3): P.ODLX = .2WORD 90,8C,84,7C + .WORD 80*2 = [288,280,264,248,256]; P.IDLX = .3WORD 8,14,1C,24 + .WORD 2C*3 = [24,60,84,108,132]; P.IIDL = .3WORD 0,10,18,28 + .WORD 40*3 = [0,48,72,120,192]. We instead use one flat WEAVE_SPEED_CAP=100 (enemy.ts:57, self-declared inferred).

The outer reversal is additionally depth-gated (RBARON.MAC:2776-2778, `LDA PLSTAT+19 / CMP I,4 / BCS 10$`), which inverts the flag in a narrow case. Our version has no such gate and also HARD-CLAMPS position (`clamp(enemy.x + deltaX, -olim, olim)`, enemy.ts:158) — the ROM never clamps position here, it only changes the delta by ACCEL.
Our ACCEL = 30 (enemy.ts:44) is a RADIX ERROR: RBARON.MAC:465 `ACCEL = 30` sits in the .RADIX 16 region (set at :74, decimal islands are :6217-6280 only), so the ROM value is 0x30 = 48.

reasoning: Attacked the structural claim by tracing register X through the loop by hand, as instructed. It holds exactly. PLNDEL:2747 `LDX I,2 ;CENTER CROSSED`; :2749 `LDA ZX,PLSTAT+8` -> PLSTAT+0A (the ROM's own comment `;PLSTAT+10.` is DECIMAL 10 = 0x0A), minus HORIZN (RBARON.MAC:456, `HORIZN = 40` in the hex region = 0x40 = 64). PASS 1 therefore runs on the Y axis: P.WINDW reads/writes PLSTAT+0C+X = PLSTAT+0E/+0F, which STPLNE:2300-2301 initialises as the Y DELTA. P.WITR:2865-2873 then does DEX/DEX (X: 2 -> 0), reloads XPOS from PLSTAT+8+0 = PLSTAT+8 (X DISPLAY), and `TXA / BMI 10$` falls through (X=0 sets Z, not N) to `JMP P.WINDW`. PASS 2 runs the identical zone/target/servo machine on the X axis (PLSTAT+0C/+0D = X DELTA, STPLNE:2308-2309). P.WITR then decrements X to 0xFE, BMI fires, RTS. Exactly two passes, Y then X — the same machine, both axes. The register DOES re-enter with a second axis, so the REFUTE condition the lead named is not met. Verified the Y integrator too: UPDPLN:2582-2611 adds PLSTAT+0E/+0F into the Y position PLSTAT+2/+3 every frame and clamps it — PFPLOW = 80*4 (RBARON.MAC:448, hex) = 0x200 = 512 and PFPHI = 140*4 (:449) = 0x500 = 1280, both exactly as claimed. Ours: enemy.ts step() spreads `...enemy` and overrides only x/deltaX/bank/depth — `y` is untouched, and there is no deltaY field at all. Flat-plane flight confirmed.
corrections: Sequencing nit for the implementer: the ROM runs the Y axis FIRST (X=2) and the X axis SECOND (X=0), and the Y axis alone is biased by HORIZN=0x40 before entering the machine (PLNDEL:2748-2754). The X axis uses the raw display position.

The Y axis reuses the SAME P.OLIM/P.ILIM/P.ODLX/P.IDLX/P.IIDL tables as X (indexed only by GMLEVL, not by axis) — so implementing EN-016's 3-zone servo correctly gives the Y axis for free by running it twice. These two findings should be built as one machine, not two.

reasoning: Re-opened P.UPD0 and it says precisely what the finding claims, verbatim. UPDPLN:2722-2726 compares the 16-bit motion depth PLSTAT+4/+5 against P.MNDP and `BPL PLNDEL ;NOT YET` — falling through to P.UPD0 only once depth < P.MNDP. P.UPD0:2727-2742 then: GMEND0 = 0C0 (`;ENABLE ENEMY & RETURNING PLANE SHELLS`, :2729-2730); MESTM2 = 10 (`;BEHIND YOU MESSAGE TIMER`, :2731-2732); ENSIDE = sign of X (:2733-2735); PLSTAT+7 = WO.RTN (:2736-2737) where RBARON.MAC:473 `WO.RTN = 10` is in the hex region = 0x10 = 16 calc frames = 16 x 96 ms = 1.536 s; PLVALU = 0 (:2739); and `STA PLSTAT+6 ;CLR PLANE` (:2741) — the object is genuinely DESTROYED, not floored. The ROM really destroys. Ours really floors: enemy.ts:161 `depth: Math.max(enemy.depth - CLOSE_SPEED, MIN_DEPTH)`. And I verified nothing downstream retires it — grepped every reassignment of `enemies` in main.ts: it is only ever rewritten by `.map(stepEnemy)` (:395), by the shot-down filter (:444), and by a new wave (:467). There is no depth-based despawn anywhere. An unshot plane parks at depth 140 and hovers. CONFIRMED.
corrections: Our MIN_DEPTH = 140 (enemy.ts:63) is a RADIX ERROR as well as a wrong mechanic: RBARON.MAC:469 `P.MNDP = 140` is in the .RADIX 16 region, so the fly-by threshold is 0x140 = 320, not 140. The plane should be retired at depth 320.

reasoning: Ran the grep myself rather than taking the auditor's word, exactly as the lead required. `grep -rnE "^\s*import .*returning-ace|from './returning-ace'|require.*returning-ace" src/` returns NOTHING (exit 1). A broader `grep -rln "returning-ace" src/` matches only two files: returning-ace.ts itself (its own header comments) and enemy.ts — and the enemy.ts hit is a COMMENT on line 62 ('the returning-ace pass past P.MNDP is rb2-8'), not an import. main.ts's import block (lines 26-52) pulls in camera, horizon, flight, enemy, waves, biplane, blimp, lives, landscape, scene, timing, guns, explosion, scoring, topology, events, audio — and never returning-ace. Zero importers. The module is genuinely dead code. This is INDEPENDENTLY corroborated by EN-018, which I confirmed separately: enemy.ts can never emit the fly-by event that would drive it, because it floors depth instead of destroying the plane. The two are consistent but not co-dependent — I verified the grep on its own terms, so the shared-wrong-assumption risk the lead flagged (vs CD-010) does not apply. ROM side also holds: MAIN calls `JSR EOLSEQ` unconditionally every calc frame (RBARON.MAC:825), EOLSEQ is at :1057.
corrections: Sizing is optimistic. The finding sizes this `m` on the assumption that only the wiring is missing, but the wiring depends on EN-018 (destroy-on-fly-by) AND on a re-entry scheduler (PLSTAT+7 = WO.RTN countdown, then NWPLNE:2250-2252 `BIT GMEND0 / BPL 15$ / JMP NWENME` re-initialises the plane as a RETURNING one) AND on the returning-plane depth integrator in UPDPLN:2674-2703, which is a separate code path from the normal one (it runs against P.MNDM = 0x11, not P.MNDP). Closer to `l` once those are counted.

reasoning: I attacked every constant in this finding independently and every single one survived — this is the most carefully decoded finding in the batch. (1) Status byte 2: RBARON.MAC:2367-2368 and :2381-2382, `LDA I,2 / STA P.1ST+6 ;FORMATION FLIGHT (=2)`. (2) Offsets: PLANE1 = .WORD -100,100 and PLANE2 = .WORD -100,-100 (:2480-2481) in the hex region -> (-256,+256) and (-256,-256). Correct. (3) The parallel->free promotion: FREPAR (:3500-3507) is called from UPDMOB:2653 on the plane-rotated-away path, and FRDRNE (:3511-3529) does `LDA ZX,PLOBDB+6 / CMP I,2 ;PARALLEL / BNE 10$ / LSR ZX,PLOBDB+6 ;FREE DRONE` (2 >> 1 = 1) and then ADDs PLSTAT/+1 and PLSTAT+2/+3 (the lead's X and Y) into the drone's stored offsets, converting them to absolute. Exactly as claimed. (4) UPDRNE (:3054-3133) is a genuinely different stepper: `LDA I,10` / `LDA I,-10` = +/-0x10 = +/-16 units/frame toward a hard destination. (5) DRPOST = .WORD 160,300,-180,380 (:3135, hex) and I checked the INDEXING, which is where this could have died: UPDRNE:3054-3058 sets SAVY = 0 for drone 0 and 4 for drone 1, then reads DRPOST+Y (X dest) and DRPOST+2+Y (Y dest). Drone 0 -> (0x160, 0x300) = (352, 768); drone 1 -> (DRPOST+4/+5, DRPOST+6/+7) = (-0x180, 0x380) = (-384, 896). The finding's pairing is correct. (6) Growth phase: :3092-3105, Z decremented by 0x60 = 96/frame down to 0x0E00, gated on DRNSFL and CPX DRNSET. Correct. (7) Screen clamp: :3117-3121, `CPY I,0C0 / SBC I,5` = 0x05C0. Correct. Our side: enemy.ts's step() never reads `kind` (the discriminant exists but is inert), waves.ts:83 bakes drones as an absolute one-time snapshot `{...lead, kind:'drone', x: lead.x+dx, y: lead.y+dy}` with no live parent link, and main.ts:395 `enemies = enemies.map((e) => stepEnemy(e, level))` puts leads and drones through the identical weave. Three steppers needed, one present.

The core claim — our 90-degree entry bank is discarded before it is ever seen — is TRUE. spawn() sets bank = side * PI/2 and deltaX = 0 (enemy.ts:134-135); step() unconditionally recomputes `bank: biplaneBank(deltaX)` (enemy.ts:160) with deltaX now +/-ACCEL, so the entry bank survives zero frames of motion. ROM side verified: STPLNE:2302-2307 sets PLSTAT+15 = 0x02 or 0xFE (with PLSTAT+14 = 0, since NWPLNE:2246-2249 zeroes the whole DB), i.e. a rotation of +/-0x0200 — and P.MAXR (RBARON.MAC:471) = 0x1FF `;90 DEGREE MAX ROTATION` corroborates that 0x200 IS 90 degrees. PLNDEL:2743-2746 `LDA PLSTAT+6 / AND I,20 / BEQ 2$ / RTS` — while D5 is set the plane does NOT weave. UPDMOB:2634-2648 rolls the rotation back at +/-0x40 = 64/frame. The entry-velocity claim also holds: MAXDEL (RBARON.MAC:470) = -180 in the hex region = -0x180 = -384, and STPLNE:2298-2309 writes it to BOTH the X delta (PLSTAT+0C/+0D) and the Y delta (PLSTAT+0E/+0F), sign-flipped by side via DPABS. We spawn with deltaX: 0. BUT the finding's timing narrative is wrong on two counts (see corrections) — it conflates two different status bits with two different clear conditions.
corrections: 'A multi-second banked arc' is WRONG. The bank itself unwinds at +/-0x40 = 64 per calc frame from 0x200 = 512, i.e. 512/64 = 8 calc frames = 0.77 s. Not multi-second. Do not implement a 3-second roll-out.

The finding conflates D4 and D5, which are separate bits with separate clears. D4 (0x10) is the ROTATION bit: UPDMOB:2631-2633 gates the roll-back on it, and UPDMOB's 25$ (:2650-2652, `AND I,0EF`) clears it once the rotation reaches zero. D5 (0x20) is the CROSS-CENTRE bit: it is cleared by FORMTN (:3543-3545, `AND I,0DF`), and NOT when the rotation hits zero — FORMTN clears it when the plane's |X display| drops below 0xE0 = 224 (:3536-3538) OR when the PLSTAT+7 counter (0x20 = 32 frames = 3.07 s) expires (:3539-3540), whichever comes FIRST. The 32 frames is a TIMEOUT, not a duration.

Missed consequence worth adding: while D4 or D5 is set, UPDMOB:2663-2666 (`AND I,30 / BEQ 35$ / JMP PLNDEL`) means the depth integrator at 35$ never runs — so the arcade plane does NOT close on the player during its entry. Ours starts boring in on frame 1.

reasoning: Both halves verified in the radix region the finding assumes. The gate: STPLNE:2325-2331 reads `LDA N.PLNZ / CMP I,4 / BCC 25$` — BCC skips the blimp entirely when N.PLNZ < 4. Only on N.PLNZ >= 4 does it roll `JSR RANDOM / AND I,0C / BNE 25$` — masking bits 2-3 gives 4 equiprobable outcomes, so the surviving branch to `JSR CINTBP ;RANDOM BLIMP` is exactly a 1-in-4 chance. So the 25% RATE matches our BLIMP_SPAWN_CHANCE = 0.25; it is only the UNLOCK GATE that is missing. N.PLNZ is the cumulative game-long plane counter, incremented at PLNZD:2398 `INC N.PLNZ ;COUNT # OF PLANES IN GAME` — the finding's line cite is exact. Our side: main.ts:471 is `if (wasDecision && blimp === null && shouldSpawnBlimp(nextFloat(blimpRng)))` — no counter gate of any kind, and I grepped src/ for any cumulative plane counter: the only hit is waves.ts:60 `planeCountForScore`, which is planes-per-wave, not planes-so-far. So no N.PLNZ analogue exists to gate on; it must be threaded in. Entry geometry also holds: INITBP:1427-1430 `LDX I,3 / LDY I,0C0 / BIT PLDELX / JSR DPABS ;-X SIDE` = |X| 0x03C0 = 960 with the side taken deterministically from the player's own turn rate, against our Rng-chosen side at |X| in [180,300) (blimp.ts:107-108, ENTRY_X_MIN=180 / ENTRY_X_RANGE=120).
corrections: Coordinate-frame caveat on the 960: INITBP adds UNIV4X into it (:1432-1438), so 0x03C0 is an offset from the UNIVERSE centre, not a screen-window X like our blimp.x. Do not port the literal 960 into blimp.ts without converting frames — the load-bearing divergence is 'deterministic from PLDELX' vs 'random', not the raw magnitude.

reasoning: Both the cadence and the level gate verified, and I checked the clock the arithmetic depends on. The blimp really does not use PLNSHL: BLMOTN:4229 is `JSR SHLAUN ;LAUNCH SHELL @ PLAYER`. SHLAUN:4027-4030 opens `LDA FRAME / AND I,3 ;1 OUT OF 4 FRAMES / STA SHMODE / BEQ SHLAU0` — a hard 1-in-4. SHLAU0 then hard-gates on level at :4038-4041, `LDX GMLEVL / DEX / DEX / BMI SHLAUX ;NO GROUND SHELLS @ LOWER LEVELS` — GMLEVL < 2 fires nothing at all. Both cites are verbatim-correct. Critically, I verified the CLOCK: FRAME is incremented at RBARON.MAC:870, inside MAIN's body (MAIN at :763, `JMP MAIN` at :878, blocking in INTWAIT at :926) — so FRAME ticks once per CALC frame, not per NMI and not per VG refresh. The finding's arithmetic therefore stands: 10.4167 Hz / 4 = 2.604 shots/s. And the contrast is real — PLNSHL, the PLANE launcher, is a separate routine at :4804 whose own cadence is `LDA FRAME` :4809 / `BCS 10$ ;EVERY OTHER FRAME` :4811, i.e. the /2 our blimp.ts borrowed. blimp.ts:145 `(Math.floor(frame) & 1) === 0` = 5.208 shots/s = exactly 2x the arcade rate, with no level gate. The finding's quotation of our own module header (blimp.ts:19-23, 'the blimp is a threat whenever it is present... a menace even in the early sky the planes leave quiet') is accurate, and the ROM says the exact reverse.
corrections: The finding understates the gating — SHLAUN has a THIRD gate it misses: :4032-4037 `LDY XPOS / LDX XPOS+1 / JSR DPABS / CPY I,1 / SBC I,3 / BCS SHLAUX ;X OFF SCREEN` blocks the shot whenever |screen X| >= 0x0301. The ROM blimp cannot shoot you while it is off to the side.

The level gate is not merely on/off: SHLAUN:4042 `LDY AX,GRSLVL` indexes a per-level SHELL BUDGET, GRSLVL = .BYTE 0,GRLSHL / .BYTE GRLSHL*3,NGRSHL (:4165-4166) — so the number of simultaneous shells also ramps with GMLEVL. A faithful fix wants the budget, not just the >= 2 cutoff.

reasoning: I re-derived every number in the fuse and the arithmetic is exactly right. INITBP:1421-1426: BLOBJ+4 = 0 (Z LSB), BLOBJ+5 = 0x10 (Z MSB) -> Z = 0x1000 = 4096. INITBP:1443-1448: `LDX I,-1 / LDY I,-8` -> 0xFFF8 = -8 units/frame X velocity, sign-flipped by entry side via DPABS. BLMOTN:4236-4242 adds that velocity into the X position each frame (the 8/frame drift). BLMOTN:4259-4265: `CLC / LDA BLOBJ+4 / ADC I,-80 / STA BLOBJ+4 / LDA BLOBJ+5 / ADC I,-1 / STA BLOBJ+5` — a 16-bit add of 0xFF80 = -128, i.e. Z falls by 128 EVERY calc frame. BLMOTN:4266-4267: `CMP I,1 / BPL 55$` on the Z MSB — when the MSB reaches 0, A-1 goes negative and it falls through to 40$ (:4268-4271) which zeroes BLOBJ+18/+6 and clears the object. So the object dies at Z < 0x100 = 256. Lifetime = (4096 - 256) / 128 = 3840 / 128 = 30 calc frames = 30 x 96 ms = 2.88 s. Every figure in the claim checks out. Closing-to-drift ratio 128:8 = 16:1, as claimed. Ours: blimp.ts:54 CRUISE_DEPTH = 600 constant, :57 DRIFT_SPEED = 12, :130 `step` returns `{...blimp, x: blimp.x + blimp.deltaX}` — depth is literally never touched. The inverse motion. And the finding's most damning point is verifiable and true: blimp.ts:30-31 asserts as fact that 'BLMOTN is not byte-transcribed in the quarry', which is false — BLMOTN is fully present at RBARON.MAC:4170-4298, and the invented motion is backwards.
corrections: 'Never expires' is wrong at the system level, though harmlessly so. blimp.ts's step() never expires it, but main.ts:409 does: `blimp = Math.abs(drifted.x) > BLIMP_DESPAWN_X ? null : drifted` with BLIMP_DESPAWN_X = 640 (main.ts:86). At 12/frame from an entry |x| of 180-300 that is ~28-38 calc frames — coincidentally close to the ROM's 30. So the LIFETIME is roughly right by accident; it is the AXIS that is inverted (lateral exit vs depth fuse). The finding's substance is untouched, but a reviewer will trip over 'never expires'.
The finding omits the blimp's Y: INITBP:1420/1423-1424 sets BLOBJ+2 = 0, BLOBJ+3 = 2 -> Y = 0x0200 = 512, a fixed altitude, not our random +/-40 (blimp.ts:64/109).

reasoning: Verified in both directions. ROM: INITBP:1422 zeroes BLOBJ+14 (Y ANGLE LSB) and :1439-1442 `LDA I,-2 / BIT TEMP1 / JSR SPABS / STA BLOBJ+15 ;Y ANGLE MSB` sets the MSB to +/-2 -> a heading of +/-0x0200 = +/-512, signed by the entry side. BLMOTN:4281-4298 unwinds it toward zero as a 16-bit value at +/-0x28 = +/-40 per calc frame (`ADC I,-28` at :4288 for a positive angle, `ADC I,28` at :4294 for a negative one, with the carry propagating into BLOBJ+15). BLMOTN:4207-4214 then feeds BLOBJ+15 through TRIG into `JSR YAXIS ;ROTATE ABOUT Y` — so it really is applied to the geometry, and really does swing the airship to face you as it closes. 512/40 = 12.8 frames to level out, exactly as the finding computes, against the ~30-frame life I independently derived in EN-025. Ours: the Blimp interface (blimp.ts:69-84) carries x, y, depth, deltaX, bank, side, active — there is no heading/yaw field, and step() (:130) touches only x. The finding's 'accept' recommendation is right-sized: this is cosmetic next to EN-024/EN-025.
corrections: 'The rotation simply does not exist in our model' overstates it slightly at the RENDER layer. main.ts:207 DOES apply a Y rotation — `multiply(rotationY(BLIMP_YAW), rotationZ(blimp.bank))` — but BLIMP_YAW is a hard-coded constant PI/2 (main.ts:78), a fixed broadside pose. So what is missing is not yaw per se but the ANIMATED unwind: a per-frame heading that starts at +/-0x200 and decays by 0x28 a frame. The fix is to replace the constant with a state field, not to add a rotation that isn't there.
